name: Latimer State Bank
description_short: Latimer State Bank is a state-chartered community bank headquartered in Wilburton, Oklahoma, offering traditional banking services with FDIC insurance protection.
description_long:
Latimer State Bank was established on April 9, 1979, and has been FDIC insured since the same date, providing nearly five decades of banking services to its community. As a state-chartered bank not affiliated with the Federal Reserve System, it operates under the oversight of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation as its primary federal regulator. The bank maintains its corporate headquarters at 301 W Main St in Wilburton, Oklahoma, serving customers in a single domestic location across one state. With FDIC Cert # 22839 and a FDIC Unique Number of 15955, the institution maintains full federal insurance coverage for eligible deposits up to established limits.

As a traditional community bank, Latimer State Bank offers standard banking products and services typical of regional financial institutions. These include deposit accounts such as checking and savings products, certificate of deposit (CD) options, and other consumer banking services. The bank operates as a localized financial institution focused on serving its immediate geographic area rather than as a multi-state or national banking operation.

Latimer State Bank distinguishes itself as a long-established community institution with nearly 45 years of continuous FDIC insurance and operation. Its status as a state-chartered bank operating independently outside the Federal Reserve System reflects a traditional community banking model. The single-location structure suggests a focused, local approach to banking rather than expansion into multiple markets.

Prospective customers should note that detailed information about specific products, interest rates, fees, and service offerings is not available through the FDIC BankFind database provided. While the bank maintains full FDIC insurance and regulatory compliance, consumers should directly contact the institution or visit their physical location to obtain comprehensive details about available services, current rates, and account terms. The limited digital footprint and single location may restrict accessibility for those preferring online or multi-location banking options.
pros: FDIC insured since 1979 with continuous federal protection for eligible deposits
Nearly 45 years of established banking operations and community presence
State-chartered status with direct FDIC oversight as primary federal regulator
Community-focused institution serving local market with personal banking relationships
Full compliance with federal banking regulations and deposit insurance standards
cons: Single location in Wilburton, Oklahoma limits accessibility for non-local customers
No evidence of online or digital banking platform from available regulatory data
Limited public information available about specific products, rates, and fees
Small single-location footprint may result in fewer services compared to larger banks
No multi-state presence or branch network for customers requiring broader access
best_for: Wilburton, Oklahoma residents seeking FDIC-insured local banking services
Customers preferring community bank relationships over large national institutions
Individuals prioritizing established, regulated financial institutions with long operational history
diagnosis: Latimer State Bank is best for Wilburton-area residents seeking traditional, FDIC-insured community banking from an established local institution. The primary caveat is that specific product offerings, rates, and digital capabilities cannot be verified from public regulatory data, requiring direct contact with the bank for detailed service information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
